prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `0b17323b`.
codex_response:
Implemented `0b17323b`. The rule is: preserve the existing color-`1` cells on the evenly spaced main diagonal, infer their step size, and continue that same progression with color-`2` until the next step would leave the grid.

Both summary hints were consistent with the official examples, so I used them as a starting check rather than discarding them; no `notes.md` or new shared primitive was needed. The generator stays close to the source family by keeping exactly three seed `1`s on a sparse main diagonal and varying board size/offset/step just enough to support 1000 unique builds. I verified `3/3` official examples, rebuilt `1000` examples successfully, and visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/0b17323b/previews/preview_01.png).
